text:
Alongside the many of the other Missouri athletics making improvements in order to be more competitive in their respective sports, swim and dive team is jumping in and building their staff.

Two-time Olympic Qualifier Brandon Loschiavo was announced as the new diving coach for Missouri. He comes with three years of experience in coaching, most recently as a volunteer assistant coach for Purdue.

"Brandon brings an incredible array of experience to the diving-well,"Mizzou Head Diving Coach Kyle Bogner stated. "Not only is his athletic experience relevant,脗聽but Brandon has been a student of the sport for many years. From strength and conditioning, to nutrition and mental performance, he has a holistic approach and is able to look at every aspect of what it takes to be a champion and apply it to a training environment. We are thrilled to have Brandon here at the Zou!"

Loschiavo has competed at the last two Olympic Games, finishing 17th in Paris this summer and 11th in the 2020 Tokyo Games for the 10-meter platform dive event.

There are no games scheduled today.

芒聙垄脗聽Missouri has planned a drone light show for the first game of the football season against Murray State. The show will occur during half time alongside Marching Mizzou. Missouri will take the field against Murray State on Aug. 29 at 7 p.m. CDT.

芒聙垄 The Track and Field team introduced a newly renovated indoor facility located at the Hearnes Center ahead of the 2024 season.

芒聙垄脗聽Volleyball will concluded preseason play with an exhibition game against Omaha on Sunday, Aug. 24. The Tigers are coming off of one their best seasons in recent history and are looking to keep the success going. Fans are invited to watch the exhibition game at the Hearnes at noon.
